review_text: Where have I seen a film like this before? No, this is Wes Anderson, whose aesthetic always thrills me. But this film—the narrative, the plot, the characters, the music, the script, the mise-en-scène—everything feels as if this story couldn't be told any other way, as if the hotel itself were an aesthetic. Fiennes and his character carry the film amazingly. It feels as if he were the embodiment of the hotel. The meta-literary interplay—a story within a story—is incredibly creative. Although I initially thought it might be pretentious, the truth is that it plays with the senses while developing human emotions that add complexity to the characters. It doesn't feel like a fairy tale; it feels like a real, relatable story: a hidden past, an undeserved legacy, a questionable employee... everything comes together dramatically.
A film that rejects the common cinema lover and immerses itself in pretentious visuals and a screenplay written by a playwright. All I want is to be told a story.
Wes Anderson delivers an iconic and cinematic masterpiece. From beautiful compositions to an all star cast delivering perfect performances. Easily a top favorite.

I guessed it was a Wes Anderson movie half way.. hahaha! As per usual, a dynamic and clever plot supported by witty dialogues and an extremely good long list of actors and actresses. I imagined that coming across such a beautiful building, The Grand Budapest Hotel, Wes couldn't avoid the temptation of a story imagining its history, life and death. A must see indeed!

Great movie, makes you feel such a sense of nostalgia. It is phenomenally shot, well written and rather funny. The ending is incredibly beautiful in such a unique way. Probably Wes Anderson's best movie.
I'm usually not a Wes Anderson fan but I think this movie is unique and perfectly directed. What a cast! The dialogue is so clever. One of Ralph Fiennes' best performances.
greatest prison, love, and travel story to ever take place in a weird barbie dream house
It was weird, which was obviously the point, and definitely one of the best from the director. Great cast.
This is a classic Wes Anderson colour palette style movie with loveable characters and a lot of fine jokes!
One of my favorite from Wes Anderson.
Visually hilarious and beautiful! Very entertaining watch, although I have to say I have never in my life met any men as competent as these fictional ones (no shade)(well,, a little shade)
i can't believe how good this is. modern classic!
From the beautiful production design to the cast funny performances, The Grand Budapest Hotel is arguably Anderson’s magnum opus.
Its an alright movie. How did the lobby boy turned from brown to white when he got older?
Must watch movie, great all the way through
Storytelling, acting, camera-work, staging, and so much more at it's peak!
Absolute gem. Wes Anderson at his best
Beautiful story within a story. Exploring the tiny but infinite detail of a lobby boy’s past life. Despite aesthetic excellence & craft, left feeling hollow.
Peak Wes Anderson. What a privilege for the cast to be preserved in this world, so future generations see what they were able to do in their prime, even in (not so) small roles.

Forget the impossible plotting and indulge in the wonderful acting and production design. Ralph Fiennes leads a characteristically quirky ensemble of veteran Wes Anderson players, some offering little more than a short line of dialogue, all at the service of a plot that involves a stolen painting, a fought over will, and several murderous characters out for revenge. It's endlessly enjoyable and completely bonkers.
Unbelievably fabulous film!

What is great about the movie is how it made, camera, lighting, music etc. The plot is not that catching. After watching it feels like the movie ended without a real highlight, but the cast is a great argument for the movie.

Probably Wes Anderson’s best film, The Grand Budapest Hotel has it all: scenic tableau shots, charmingly eccentric characters, and melancholy humor that will keep you thinking about the film for the next few days after watching.
Never understood the hype around this. Performances were good but this whole world seems odd to me. I get that, that’s the intention but it’s just not for me

The tender and pure relationship between Gustave and Zero, the Lobby Boy, is infectious and endearing. The set design, the costumes, the cinematography, the quirky dialogue, everything about this movie screams Wes Anderson... and I love it. I've seen this movie called his magnum opus and I completely agree. If I was asked to recommend a Wes Anderson movie to someone, this would be at the top of the list. The honesty and unabashed approach to every aspect of this film is what endears me to it so much. It's simple, it's complicated and it's one of my new favorites. Best Character: Gustave Best Quote: "There are still faint glimmers of civilization left in this barbaric slaughterhouse that was once known as humanity... He was one of them. What more is there to say?" - Mr. Moustafa Best Scene: The snow chase and cliff Best Piece of Score: "Mr. Moustafa" Watch filtered on VidAngel
Might not be as good as lord of the rings (what is?) but this is still a fiver in my book.
One of my favorite all times!

Always enjoy the quirkiness, dialogue and unique sets for Wes Anderson movies. Ralph Fiennes along with his amazing supporting cast make this movie a very enjoyable watch. My favorite of Wes Andersons films.

Probably one of my all-time favourite movies! Well-designed visuals with beautiful execution, fantastic casting, very moving story AND music...what else can I say? This has been by far the absolute highlight of Wes's career and he probably will never be able to top this masterpiece. Well done to the whole cast and crew!
Yes, most definitely, yes.
Ralph put on a masterclass. Every moment with him was captivating. Loved the story, Wes's best in my personal opinion. Dafoe slayed as always.

Deep in unexpected ways. Obviously -or not if you don't like Wes' style ahaha- beautiful ,colours,construction, opening minds to David Hockney. And ,fck me, humor. I 4& 1/2 stared " The Phoenician Scheme " for the same reasons. I suppose.
This movie is so entertaining! The aesthetic and the story were so beautifully crafted that I never wanted this movie to end. I definitely recommend it for comedy fans!

It's been a couple of days now since I watched this film, but it's definitely gotten me in the mood to watch Wes Anderson's new film, and it also makes me appreciate why so many people love this film. Now, I've got a feeling that part of the reason this didn't set me alight was purely down to the fact that I was tired while watching this film and I did struggle to stay awake, however that had more to do with having done 9 days straight at work, as opposed to the film itself. I really enjoyed both the audio and visual gags in the film, and one that sticks with me is the moment at the start where Misure Jean, instead of opening the bar flap; decided to duck underneath the counter and then run towards the patron who was suffering. This made me audibly laugh and is something that will stick with me forever. I also really enjoyed the use of forced perspective and alternative perspectives. It helped to give the film a completely different vibe to anything I've seen recently and really helped to keep the focus on the story itself as opposed to flashy visuals. Although, I'd argue they were flashy in their own unique way. Definitely worth a watch and who knows... maybe one day I'll re-watch it when I'm not trying to keep my head up as I'm sitting in the cinema like a nodding dog! Oh and for the avoidance of doubt, yes my local cinema was re-showing Wes Anderson's films in the run up to the release of the Phoenecian Sceheme which I can't wait for.
Very annoying and cringey movie
An utter delight of a film that I continue to revisit on a regular basis. It might actually be Wes Anderson's best movie, which is saying quite a lot.

Ralph Fiennes' performance is a tour-de-force and the whole movie is a ton of fun. The stop-motion bits are hilarious and the plot is so absurd it is hilarious. I didn't much like the unnecessary epilogue though. But this is still my second favorite Wes Anderson film after Life Aquatic.

Masterpiece! Lost count of how many times I saw it. Incredible journey through a weird and wonderful story, fascinating all the way. Quirky and sophisticated at the same time. Unique in so many ways, the cinematography is something else. The whole cast really shines in character. Fast moving, lots of twists and turns, unpredictable, highly entertaining. Just love it ...
Still one of the best shows of all time, imho. So well done!

A meticulously crafted romp, The Grand Budapest Hotel is Wes Anderson at his most visually confident and narratively efficient. The film’s whimsical style never undercuts its momentum — in fact, it sharpens it. Every frame is packed with detail, but it’s the quick wit, crisp pacing, and rich performances that make this one of Anderson’s most satisfying works. Ralph Fiennes gives a career-best comic performance, anchoring the film’s pastel-colored world with elegance, vanity, and vulnerability. Sleek, hilarious, and slyly emotional — it’s a rare blend that sticks.

A film that fills you with joy when you watch it. A fantastic and slightly fantastical combination of live action, stop motion and color in both art and character. A movie anyone can watch with subtle happiness swelling inside their heart.
Good visuals , but the story didn’t resonate with me , I think it was trying to be charming but it didn’t work for me.
This film uses fancy visuals to explore deep feelings.

Great performance by Fiennes. Anderson's life action isn't really my cup of tea (exception being The Life Aquatic with Steve Zissou). His animated features are a better fit for his style of directing.
The story is fun, the filming is so stylish.

Maravilhosamente poético, baseado na fantástica obra do romancista austríaco judeu Stefan Zweig, que em 1940 fugiu para Petrópolis, Brasil, onde faleceu aos 60 anos (1942), adoro esses contos com fotografia retro, narração ágil e divertida, um pequeno mistério investigativo, com doses extras de drama, trágico e engraçado, nos faz rir e emociona, excelente produção, caprichosa, delicada e lírica, como todas as obras dirigidas por Wes Anderson recheada de fantasia e magia… Adorável...

Another unique film from the mind of Wes Anderson. With beautiful visuals and different characters. I really enjoyed the pacing of this movie as it never had a lull and I felt all time was being put to use. The cast was star studded and provided excellent performances. I liked the humor of this movie as it still had its dramatic moments but never took itself to seriously. I really did enjoy this one and I do recommend it to others. I just think there was a few moments when it fell flat but I believe that is because my humor didn't line up 100% with the movies.
An old fashion comedy that has some great visuals and has such a unique look that leaves me off with a memorable experience.

I don’t really know what the movie was about since I wasn’t here for all of it but what I did like about the small portion I watched was the long shot film where there was people eating and all in uniform. I also really enjoyed the setting it was filmed like where they robbed the painting with the man and a apple and I also liked the colors from the movie.
